title: Canopy and cavern crossover phase
summary: Sixty-sixth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-3 wave makes the forest's top and bottom families feel more like one coherent destination, then adds one small recovery-or-memory follow-on.

execution_order.why_now: The forest vertical family is ready to read as one destination instead of separate showcases.
A stronger crossover can deepen the forest outing more than more raw depth would.
The family should cohere without getting harder or noisier.
A small follow-on can make the whole family easier to remember and re-enter.
The vertical family is now strong enough to benefit from a warmer return seam.
The outing family should deepen without another shell or cue language.

guardrails: Do not add a larger forest traversal rewrite.
Do not add another cue language.
Reuse existing canopy, cave, and recovery seams.
acceptance_focus: The forest vertical family feels more like one destination outing.
Recovery or memory payoff stays compact and useful.
Lane 3 strengthens cohesion instead of raw size.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-canopy-and-cavern-crossover-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 3's later long-push wave after the tundra relief work: make the forest canopy and cavern spaces feel more like one coherent destination without a traversal rewrite or a new cue language.
